title: Theme and Settings Management
description: Applying themes, saving user preferences, registering settings tabs

use_cases.name: Apply Custom Theme Colors
use_cases.trigger: User adjusts color picker in UIWindowThemeDialog
steps: UIWindowThemeDialog calls svc_theme.apply({ hue, sat, lig, alpha, light_text })
ThemeService.apply() merges values into state, calls reload_() then save_()
reload_() sets CSS custom properties on :root (--primary-hue, --primary-saturation, etc.)
reload_() calls BroadcastService.sendBroadcast('themeChanged', palette, { sendToNewAppInstances: true })
save_() debounces (1000ms) then calls commit_save_() → puter.fs.write('~/.__puter_gui.json', JSON)

use_cases.name: Reset Theme to Defaults
use_cases.trigger: User clicks 'Reset Colors' button in UIWindowThemeDialog
steps: UIWindowThemeDialog calls svc_theme.reset()
ThemeService.reset() restores default_values {sat:41.18, hue:210, lig:93.33, alpha:0.8, light_text:false}
Calls reload_() → updates CSS vars + broadcasts themeChanged
Calls puter.fs.delete('~/.__puter_gui.json') to remove saved preferences

use_cases.name: Load Saved Theme on Startup
steps: ThemeService._init() calls puter.fs.read('~/.__puter_gui.json')
If file exists: parses JSON, merges data.colors into state, calls reload_()
If file missing (code subject_does_not_exist): silently ignores
Also resolves BroadcastService via globalThis.services.get('broadcast')

use_cases.name: Broadcast Theme Change to Running Apps
use_cases.trigger: Called from reload_() after any theme change
steps: BroadcastService.sendBroadcast('themeChanged', { palette }, { sendToNewAppInstances: true })
Iterates all .window-app-iframe[data-appUsesSDK=true] elements
Posts message { msg:'broadcast', name:'themeChanged', data:palette } to each iframe via postMessage
Stores broadcast in #broadcastsToSendToNewAppInstances Map for future app launches

use_cases.name: Send Saved Broadcasts to Newly Launched App
steps: BroadcastService.sendSavedBroadcastsTo(appInstanceID) is called
Locates iframe by appInstanceID selector
Iterates #broadcastsToSendToNewAppInstances and posts each saved message to the new app

use_cases.name: Open Settings Window
steps: UIWindowSettings calls svc_settings.get_tabs() → returns #tabs array
Renders sidebar items and content panels from tab metadata (id, icon, title_i18n_key)
Each tab may have factory, dom, or html() for rendering content

use_cases.name: Register Settings Tab
steps: SettingsService._init() calls register_tab() for: UsageTab, AccountTab, SecurityTab, PersonalizationTab, LanguageTab, KeyboardShortcutsTab, AboutTab
register_tab(tab) accepts plain tab object or UIElement
If UIElement: extracts metadata via ui_element.as(TSettingsTab).get_metadata()
Pushes tab descriptor into #tabs array

use_cases.name: Format Duration for Display
use_cases.trigger: Display of time-based values (e.g., storage usage)
steps: LocaleService.format_duration(seconds) converts seconds to HH:MM:SS or MM:SS or SS format
Uses i18n('seconds') for localized label when only seconds remain
Returns formatted string for display in UI

key_relationships: ThemeService depends_on UIAlert (error display on parse failure)
SettingsService.register_tab() uses TSettingsTab trait for UIElement metadata extraction
BroadcastService sends postMessage to App Instance iframes
LocaleService depends_on i18n module for localized labels
important_notes: ThemeService uses a 1s debounce before persisting to filesystem. BroadcastService caches broadcasts for new app instances. SettingsService supports dynamic tab registration (plugin pattern) via TSettingsTab trait. LocaleService is minimal — only format_duration() method. All services extend base Service class from definitions.js.
